steps.title: Recognize the required language type
steps.content: The goal demands emphasizing release ORDER — the temporal relationship between 1928 and 1943. This requires language that puts the passage of time in focus.

steps.title: Match language type to the choices
steps.content:
A uses "was released early" and "came out years later" to put the temporal sequence in focus — exactly what the goal calls for.

  • B — "Merle Oberon was famous for her roles in films like The Three Passions (1928) and Forever and a Day (1943)." Lists both films but uses "famous for" framing that emphasizes fame, not the order of their release.
  • C — "Early in her career, Merle Oberon wasn't listed in some film credits, such as the credits for the film The Three Passions, where she played a nameless, uncredited role." Mentions only one film and emphasizes credit status, not the temporal order between the two films.
  • D — "In The Three Passions (1928), actress Merle Oberon played a nameless, uncredited role; however, in Forever and a Day (1943), she played a credited role — that of Marjorie Ismay." Addresses both films but emphasizes the contrast between uncredited and credited roles rather than the temporal order of their release.

A is correct.
